title: A variable which is not modified shall be const qualified
description: A variable which is not modified shall be const qualifiedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellmisra-c++2008Why is this an issue?More InfoThis rule is a strict implementation of a MISRA (Motor Industry Software Reliability Association) rule. MISRA defines best practices for developing safety-critical software. You can learn more about this rule in the MISRA documents referenced below.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Variable should be const qualified if it is not modified
checks.details: Variables that are not modified after initialization should be declared as const for better code safety and clarity.
